manual_entries.title: Boiler Plant Enable Logic
manual_entries.summary: Enable the boiler plant in the lowest stage when disabled for at least 15 minutes, number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests exceeds I, OAT is below HW-LOT, and the enable schedule is active.

structured_payload.title: Boiler Plant Enable Logic
structured_payload.summary: Enable the boiler plant in the lowest stage when disabled for at least 15 minutes, number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests exceeds I, OAT is below HW-LOT, and the enable schedule is active.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes, Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests > I (default 0, adjustable), OAT < HW-LOT, and boiler plant enable schedule is active.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Enable the plant in the lowest stage.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, adjustable), and b. OAT<HW-LOT, and c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is active.

When a Fault Condition is reported to the operator, it shall be a Level 3 alarm and shall include the
description of the fault and the  list of possible diagnoses from the table in Section 5.20.18.6 .

MinPriPumpSpdStage.
5.21.2.  Plant Enable/Disable
5.21.2.1.  The Boiler plant shall include an enabling schedule that allows operators to lock out the plant
during off -hours, e.g. to allow off -hour operation of HVAC systems  except the Boiler plant. The
default schedule shall be 24/7 (adjustable).
5.21.2.2.  Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and:

manual_entries.title: Boiler Plant Disable Logic
manual_entries.summary: Disable the boiler plant when enabled for at least 15 minutes and either number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, OAT > HW-LOT + 1°F, or the enable schedule is inactive.

structured_payload.title: Boiler Plant Disable Logic
structured_payload.summary: Disable the boiler plant when enabled for at least 15 minutes and either number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, OAT > HW-LOT + 1°F, or the enable schedule is inactive.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Plant has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and (Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes OR OAT > HW-LOT + 1°F OR boiler plant enable schedule is inactive).
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT>HW-LOT + 1°F, or c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is inactive.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Number of Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, ad justable),
and
b. OAT<HW -LOT, and
c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is active.
5.21.2.3.  Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and:
a. Number of Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or
b. OAT>HW -LOT + 1°F, or
c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is inactive.
Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests are generated by coil control valves. If the plant serves critical valves whose
positions are not known to the plant controller, e.g. pneumatic controls, the Heating Hot -Water Plant Request
variable can be set to 1 manually by the operator such that the plant is enabled strictly based on OAT lockout and
schedule per subsequent logic.
Importance multipliers (IM) shall be added to Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests in a future addendum to Guideline
36 to ensure that critical coils can independently cause the plant to start.

c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is active.

evidence.evidence_text: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT>HW-LOT + 1°F, or c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is inactive.

manual_entries.title: Boiler Minimum Stage Runtime
manual_entries.summary: Each boiler stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Boiler Minimum Stage Runtime
structured_payload.summary: Each boiler stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: A stage change has just completed.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Prevent any further stage changes for 10 minutes.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Staging events require that a boiler stage be available. A stage shall be deemed unavailable  if the
stage cannot be achieved because a boiler required to operate in the stage is faulted per Section
5.1.15.5.b.1.iii  or a hot water pump dedicated to that boil er is faulted per Section 5.1.15.5.b.1.i ;
otherwise the stage shall be deemed available.
5.21.3.9.  Staging shall be executed per the conditions below subject t o the following requirements:
a. Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.
b. Timers shall reset to zero at the completion of every stage change..
chunk.text_excerpt:
Staging events require that a boiler stage be available. A stage shall be deemed unavailable  if the
stage cannot be achieved because a boiler required to operate in the stage is faulted per Section
5

evidence.evidence_text: Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.

manual_entries.title: Boiler Stage Down – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
manual_entries.summary: Stage down if Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes OR the minimum flow bypass valve is >0% open for 5 minutes, the failsafe stage up condition is not true, and Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity of the next lower stage for 5 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Boiler Stage Down – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
structured_payload.summary: Stage down if Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes OR the minimum flow bypass valve is >0% open for 5 minutes, the failsafe stage up condition is not true, and Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity of the next lower stage for 5 minutes.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: (Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN of current stage for 5 min OR minimum flow bypass valve >0% open for 5 min) AND failsafe stage up condition not true AND Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of next lower stage for 5 min.
configurable_values: Design capacity of boilers in next lower stage
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail safe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.

chunk.cleaned_text:

g. Stage down if all of the following are true:
1. Either:
i. Qrequired  falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or

Condensing boilers are generally more efficient at low load since the ratio of heat transfer surface area to thermal
mass flowrate is maximized, increasing flue gas condensation. Staging on boilers at low load therefore maximizes.

manual_entries.title: Boiler Stage Up – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
manual_entries.summary: Stage up when Qrequired exceeds 200% (primary only) or 150% (variable primary) of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes and hot water flowrate exceeds the minimum flow setpoint of the next stage.

structured_payload.title: Boiler Stage Up – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Condensing boiler plant (primary only or variable primary/variable secondary)
structured_payload.summary: Stage up when Qrequired exceeds 200% (primary only) or 150% (variable primary) of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes and hot water flowrate exceeds the minimum flow setpoint of the next stage.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Qrequired > threshold * B-STAGE MIN of next available stage for 10 minutes AND hot water flowrate > minimum flow setpoint of next stage.
configurable_values: Threshold (200% for primary only, 150% for variable primary)
Minimum flow setpoint of next stage
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Efficiency Condition: Both of the following are true: i. Qrequired exceeds 200% of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes ii. Hot water flowrate exceeds the minimum flow setpoint of the next available stage (see Section 5.21.8).

238 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  1. Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage is
unavailable. The availability condition is not subject to the minimum stage runtime
requirement. Or
2. Efficiency Condition: Qrequired  exceeds 90% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in
the current stage for 10 minutes; or
3. Failsafe Condition: HW supply temperature is 10°F < setpoint for 15 minutes.
k. Stage down if both of the following are true:

minutes; and
2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true.
Non-condensing boilers do not benefit significantly from operating at low turndowns since the primary benefit of
doing so is to maximiz e condensing, which is not permissible with non -condensing boiler heat exchangers. Logic is
therefore simplified by running boilers to near full output prior to staging.
Retain the following four section s for hybrid boiler plants.

The following logic is written with the intent that the designer first enables all condensing boiler stages before any
non-condensing boiler stages. The logic will still work if this rule is not followed, but some of the efficiency afforded
by the condensing boilers may be lost in the process.
l. If all boilers enabled in the next higher stage are condensing, stage up if any of the following is
true:
1. Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage is
unavailable. The ava ilability condition is not subject to the minimum stage runtime
requirement. Or
2. Efficiency Condition: Both of the following are true:
i. Qrequired  exceeds 150% of B-STAGE MIN of the next available stage for 10 minutes
ii. Primary hot water flowrate exceeds the min imum flow setpoint of the next available
stage (see Section 5.21.8 ).
3. Failsafe Condition:  HW supply temperature is 10°F < setpoint for 15 minutes.
m.

manual_entries.title: Hot Water Supply Temperature Reset
manual_entries.summary: Reset plant hot water supply temperature setpoint using Trim & Respond logic with specified parameters.

structured_payload.title: Hot Water Supply Temperature Reset
structured_payload.summary: Reset plant hot water supply temperature setpoint using Trim & Respond logic with specified parameters.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Apply Trim & Respond logic to reset HWST setpoint between SPmin and SPmax.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Plant hot water supply temperature setpoint shall be reset using Trim & Respond logic with the following parameters: Variable Value Device Any HW Pump Distribution Loop SP0 SPmax SPmin 90ºF for condensing and hybrid boiler plants; 155° F for non-condensing plants SPmax HWSTmax Td 10 minutes T 5 minutes I 2 R Hot-Water Reset Requests SPtrim -2ºF SPres +3ºF SPres-max +7ºF

isolation valve.

manual_entries.title: Non-Condensing Boiler Condensation Control – Primary Only
manual_entries.summary: A reverse acting P-only loop resets minimum flow bypass valve position from 0% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to 100% at 135°F.

structured_payload.title: Non-Condensing Boiler Condensation Control – Primary Only
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Primary-only hot water plant with non-condensing boilers
structured_payload.summary: A reverse acting P-only loop resets minimum flow bypass valve position from 0% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to 100% at 135°F.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Enable the condensation control loop. Reset MinCondVlvPos linearly between 0% and 100% as boiler entering temperature drops from 140°F to 135°F.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: A reverse acting condensation control P-only loop shall reset a required minimum flow bypass valve position variable, MinCondVlvPos, from 0% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to 100% at 135°F boiler entering temperature.

chunk.cleaned_text:
244 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.21.5.1.  A reverse act ing condensation control P -only loop shall reset a required minimum flow bypass
valve position variable, MinCondVlvPos, from 0% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to 100% at
135°F boiler entering temperature.
5.21.5.2.  Minimum bypass valve condensation control loo p shall be enabled whenever any non -condensing
boiler is enabled. Loop shall be disabled otherwise.
Retain the following two section s for Primary -secondary plants with constant speed primary pumps and
variable speed secondary pumps. Delete otherwise.
5.21.5.3.  A rev erse acting condensation control P -only loop shall reset an allowable maximum secondary
pump speed variable, MaxSecCondSpd, from 100% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to
minimum pump speed at 135°F boiler entering temperature.
5.21.5.4.  Secondary pump speed cond ensation control loop shall be enabled whenever any secondary pump
is enabled and any non -condensing boiler is enabled.
chunk.text_excerpt:
244 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.21.5.1.  A reverse act ing condensation control P -only loop shall reset a required minimum flow bypass
valve position variable, MinCondVlvPos, from 0% at 140°F boiler

evidence.evidence_text: A reverse acting condensation control P-only loop shall reset a required minimum flow bypass valve position variable, MinCondVlvPos, from 0% at 140°F boiler entering temperature to 100% at 135°F boiler entering temperature.

manual_entries.title: HW Plant – Low Boiler Leaving Temperature Alarm
manual_entries.summary: If boiler leaving water temperature is more than 15°F below setpoint for more than 15 minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload.title: HW Plant – Low Boiler Leaving Temperature Alarm
structured_payload.summary: If boiler leaving water temperature is more than 15°F below setpoint for more than 15 minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload.fault_condition: Boiler leaving water temperature > 15°F below setpoint for > 15 minutes after boiler enabled for > 15 minutes.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Low boiler leaving hot water temperature (more than 15°F below setpoint) for more than 15 minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes: Level 3

chunk.cleaned_text:
Maintenance interval alarm when b oiler has operated for more than 2000 hours as indicated by the
Staging Runtime:  Level 4. Reset the Staging Runtime interval counter when alarm is
acknowledged.
5.21.10.3.  Boiler alarm:  Level 2
5.21.10.4.  Low boiler leaving hot water temperature (more than 15°F below setpoint ) for more than 15
minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes:  Level 3
5.21.10.5.  Pump alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15
seconds.
a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until  the equipment has been
commanded on for 15 seconds.
b. Commanded off, status on:  Level 4. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been
commanded off for 60 seconds.

otherwise.

acknowledged.

evidence.evidence_text: Low boiler leaving hot water temperature (more than 15°F below setpoint) for more than 15 minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes: Level 3

manual_entries.title: HW Plant – Pump Alarm
manual_entries.summary: Pump alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds: commanded on but status off is Level 2 (delay 15 sec); commanded off but status on is Level 4 (delay 60 sec).

structured_payload.title: HW Plant – Pump Alarm
structured_payload.summary: Pump alarm if status differs from command for 15 seconds: commanded on but status off is Level 2 (delay 15 sec); commanded off but status on is Level 4 (delay 60 sec).
structured_payload.fault_condition: Status differs from command for 15 seconds.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Pump alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded on for 15 seconds. b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been commanded off for 60 seconds.

chunk.cleaned_text:
Maintenance interval alarm when b oiler has operated for more than 2000 hours as indicated by the
Staging Runtime:  Level 4. Reset the Staging Runtime interval counter when alarm is
acknowledged.
5.21.10.3.  Boiler alarm:  Level 2
5.21.10.4.  Low boiler leaving hot water temperature (more than 15°F below setpoint ) for more than 15
minutes when boiler has been enabled for longer than 15 minutes:  Level 3
5.21.10.5.  Pump alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command for 15
seconds.
a. Commanded on, status off:  Level 2. Do not evaluate alarm until  the equipment has been
commanded on for 15 seconds.
b. Commanded off, status on:  Level 4. Do not evaluate alarm until the equipment has been
commanded off for 60 seconds.

otherwise.

manual_entries.title: HW Plant – FC#6: Hot Water Supply Temperature Too Low
manual_entries.summary: Fault condition: HWST AVG + ƐHWT < HWSTSP. Possible causes: mechanical problem with boilers, primary flow higher than design, boiler sensor out of calibration.

structured_payload.title: HW Plant – FC#6: Hot Water Supply Temperature Too Low
structured_payload.summary: Fault condition: HWST AVG + ƐHWT < HWSTSP. Possible causes: mechanical problem with boilers, primary flow higher than design, boiler sensor out of calibration.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation HWST AVG + ƐHWT < HWSTSP Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water supply temperature is too low. Possible Diagnosis Mechanical problem with boilers Primary flow is higher than the design flow of the operating boilers Deviation between the internal boiler hot water supply temperature sensor and the plant hot water supply temperature is too high (i.e. boiler sensor is out of calibration).

manual_entries.title: HW Plant – FC#8: Return Temperature Too High for Condensing
manual_entries.summary: Fault condition: HWRT AVG - ƐHWT > CondTemp. Possible causes: HWST setpoint too high, load too low, high bypass flow raising entering water temperature, coils not designed for condensing.

structured_payload.title: HW Plant – FC#8: Return Temperature Too High for Condensing
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Hot water plant with condensing boilers
structured_payload.summary: Fault condition: HWRT AVG - ƐHWT > CondTemp. Possible causes: HWST setpoint too high, load too low, high bypass flow raising entering water temperature, coils not designed for condensing.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: FC#8 Equation HWRT AVG - ƐHWT > CondTemp Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water return temperature is too high for condensing to occur. Possible Diagnosis Hot water supply temperature setpoint is too high. Hot water load is too low. High bypass flow is raising the entering water temperature. Hot water coils are not designed for condensing at current loads.

manual_entries.title: HW Plant – FC#9: Return Temperature Too Low (Condensing Risk)
